hero.title: About JARVIS 2-Flow
hero.subtitle: OpenClaw-level AI assistant built for mobile and desktop. Transform your old phone into a powerful AI assistant.

mission.title: Our Mission
paragraphs: Every year, millions of smartphones end up in landfills. At the same time, powerful AI models like Claude, GPT, and Llama are becoming increasingly capable but remain inaccessible to many due to cost barriers.

MyLittleJarvis bridges this gap. We believe your old phone deserves a second life as your personal AI assistant—handling your scheduling, coding alongside you, managing your digital life, all while you save money and reduce e-waste.

members.name: AIR
members.desc: Multi-Agent Orchestration expert. Believes AI should be accessible to everyone.

members.name: JARVIS Core Team
members.desc: A growing community of developers passionate about democratizing AI technology.

items.title: Accessibility
items.desc: AI for everyone, not just those who can afford expensive subscriptions.

items.title: Sustainability
items.desc: Reduce e-waste by extending the life of existing devices.

items.title: Privacy First
items.desc: Your data stays on your device. No cloud surveillance.

items.title: Innovation
items.desc: Push the boundaries of what old hardware can achieve with modern AI.

items.title: Instant Access
items.desc: Install with one command: npm install -g my-little-jarvis-cli. No Telegram required. Run jarvis and you're ready.

hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know about MyLittleJarvis. Can't find what you're looking for? Check the documentation or contact us.

questions.question: What devices are supported?
questions.answer: MyLittleJarvis supports any Android phone running Android 8.0+ with at least 3GB RAM. We also support Raspberry Pi 4/5, and any Linux device that can run Docker or Python. iOS support is coming soon!

questions.question: Do I need an internet connection?
questions.answer: Yes, for the initial setup and for cloud-based AI models. However, if you use local models via Ollama, you can run completely offline after setup. Some features like web search require internet connectivity.

questions.question: Is my data private?
questions.answer: Absolutely! Your data never leaves your device unless you explicitly configure cloud integrations. We use local storage for memory, local AI models via Ollama for offline operation, and end-to-end encryption for any cloud communications.

questions.question: How much does it cost?
questions.answer: MyLittleJarvis is completely free and open source! You only pay for API calls if you choose to use cloud-based AI models like Claude or GPT-4. Using local models with Ollama costs nothing beyond electricity.

questions.question: Can I use it for coding?
questions.answer: Yes! JARVIS includes Claude Code integration, allowing you to code alongside a powerful AI assistant. It can read files, suggest improvements, debug errors, and even execute commands—all at a fraction of the cost of Claude Pro.

questions.question: How do I install without Telegram?
questions.answer: Use the CLI! Run npm install -g my-little-jarvis-cli, then jarvis --setup to configure your API keys. After setup, just type jarvis to start chatting directly in your terminal—no Telegram bot required. The CLI is the recommended way to get started.

cta.title: Still have questions?
cta.subtitle: Can't find the answer you're looking for? Check out our documentation or reach out to the community.

install.title: Installation
install.content:
Install JARVIS in minutes using npm. No complex setup required.

## Prerequisites
- Node.js 22.0.0 or higher
- npm or yarn
- (Optional) Ollama for local models

## Quick Install

### Step 1: Install via npm
```bash
npm install -g my-little-jarvis-cli
```

### Step 2: Run Setup Wizard
```bash
jarvis --setup
```
The interactive wizard will guide you through:
1. Telegram Bot configuration (optional)
2. Claude API key (optional, for cloud features)
3. Ollama installation (for local models)
4. Persona selection

### Step 3: Start Chatting
```bash
jarvis
```

## Platform-Specific Notes

### macOS
Works with both Intel and Apple Silicon. Install Node.js via Homebrew:
```bash
brew install node
```

### Linux
Tested on Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 11+, and most modern distributions. For Ollama:
```bash
curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
```

### Windows (WSL)
Install WSL2 and Ubuntu, then follow Linux instructions above.

## Verification
After installation, verify everything works:
```bash
jarvis --version
jarvis status
```

## Updating
To update to the latest version:
```bash
npm update -g my-little-jarvis-cli
```

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.content:
System requirements for running JARVIS CLI.

## Minimum Requirements

### Node.js
- **Version**: 22.0.0 or higher
- **Memory**: 4GB RAM minimum (8GB+ recommended)
- **Storage**: 500MB for JARVIS + 2-8GB per AI model

### Operating System
- **macOS**: 10.15 (Catalina) or higher
- **Linux**: Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 11+, CentOS 8+
- **Windows**: Windows 10/11 with WSL2

## For Local AI Models (Ollama)

### Minimum
- **RAM**: 8GB (for smaller models like qwen2.5:1.5b)
- **Storage**: 2GB per small model

### Recommended
- **RAM**: 16GB+ (for larger models like phi3:mini)
- **Storage**: 8GB for multiple models
- **GPU**: Optional but recommended for faster inference

## For Cloud AI (Claude)
- **Internet**: Stable connection required
- **API Key**: Anthropic API key (optional)
- **Cost**: Pay-per-use (typically $0.01-0.05 per request)

## Network Requirements
- Outbound HTTPS (port 443) for:
  - npm registry (installation)
  - Anthropic API (optional cloud features)
  - Ollama model downloads (optional)

## Browser (Optional)
For web dashboard features:
- Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+

cli.title: CLI Guide
cli.content:
JARVIS CLI provides a powerful terminal interface for interacting with your AI assistant. All commands work seamlessly across macOS, Linux, and Windows (WSL).

## Installation
```bash
npm install -g my-little-jarvis-cli
```

## Commands

### jarvis (or jarvis chat)
Start interactive chat session. This is the default command.
```bash
jarvis
```
Once started, type your messages and JARVIS will respond. Use /help for available commands.

### jarvis setup
Run the interactive setup wizard for first-time configuration.
```bash
jarvis setup
```
The wizard will guide you through:
- Telegram Bot configuration (optional)
- Claude API key setup (optional)
- Ollama local models installation
- Default persona selection

### jarvis config
View and edit your configuration.
```bash
jarvis config              # View current config
jarvis config --set KEY=VALUE   # Update a setting
```

### jarvis status
Check system health and connection status.
```bash
jarvis status
```
Shows:
- Ollama connection status
- Claude API availability
- Model loading status
- Memory usage

### jarvis models
List available AI models and their status.
```bash
jarvis models
```

### jarvis memory
View conversation history and manage memory.
```bash
jarvis memory              # View history
jarvis memory --clear      # Clear all history
```

## Interactive Chat Commands
When in chat mode, type:
- `/help` - Show available commands
- `/reset` - Clear conversation history
- `/persona [name]` - Switch AI persona
- `/model [name]` - Switch AI model
- `/exit` - Quit chat

## Environment Variables
Create a `.env` file in your project root:
```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=123456:ABC-...
OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
```
